desc: Hội An is a lantern-lit riverside heritage town with excellent food and easy beach access, and it is typically most comfortable from February through April before peak heat and heavy autumn rains.
hls: UNESCO-listed Old Town of yellow merchant houses, assembly halls, and temples
Lantern-filled evening atmosphere along the Thu Bồn riverfront
Outstanding regional cuisine, street snacks, and cooking-class options
Easy bike or taxi hops to An Bàng and Cửa Đại beaches
Strong tailor and artisan workshop scene for custom clothing and crafts
Best weather window usually February to April; wettest period often September to November

todo.name: Walk the UNESCO Old Town at dawn
todo.desc: Start early for softer light, cooler lanes, and quieter bridges before day-tour groups arrive.

todo.name: Boat ride and lantern hour on the river
todo.desc: Take a short evening ride for illuminated waterfront views and classic photo angles.

todo.name: Street-food crawl in Hội An market area
todo.desc: Sample cao lầu, white rose dumplings, and mì Quảng on a guided or self-led route.

todo.name: Cycling loop to An Bàng Beach
todo.desc: Ride a flat route through villages and rice fields, then cool off by the coast.

todo.name: Half-day trip to Mỹ Sơn Sanctuary
todo.desc: Visit Cham-era temple ruins with a morning departure to avoid midday heat.

todo.name: Tailor fitting + return visit
todo.desc: Plan at least two fittings over 24–48 hours for custom garments and final adjustments.

wifi.note: Old Town hotels, cafés, and coworking spots are generally reliable; speeds dip during storms and island beach areas.

prac.fltNote: No direct London-Gateway service to Da Nang; typical UK routings connect via Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City, Bangkok, Singapore, or Gulf hubs.
prac.currency: Vietnamese đồng (VND); cash remains useful for markets, taxis, and smaller eateries.
prac.lang: Vietnamese (English widely used in tourism services).
prac.visa: Vietnam e-visa rules and allowed stay lengths vary by nationality; verify before booking flights.
alerts: September to November can bring very heavy rain and localized flooding in low Old Town streets.
Heat/humidity rise sharply from late spring into summer; plan midday breaks and hydration.
Tailor quality varies—check fabric specs and fit timelines before paying deposits.

sweet: February to April for the strongest mix of comfortable weather, low flood risk, and enjoyable evening walks.
